_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for pages/scenario/bat-city.html. Driven by render_bat_city.py.
_meta.notes: Studio profile + 5-mentor team. Content lifted verbatim from the old scenario page (Card 1 + Card 2).

pageTitle: Bat City Collective | IS2053 Programming I

aboutTheStudio.label: About the Studio
aboutTheStudio.intro_html:
Welcome to Bat City Collective — Austin’s premier indie game studio. Founded in 2019 by developers who escaped the crushing world of AAA game studio crunch culture, Bat City has one mission: make great games without burning out.

Our studio specializes in retro-style games, text adventures, and nostalgia-driven experiences. We’re 15 people working out of a converted warehouse in East Austin.

openingMentor.name: Danny Cavanaugh
openingMentor.quote_html: “Our company tagline says it all: Same Bat-code. Same Bat-channel. We believe great games are built brick by brick, with patience, craftsmanship, and a whole lot of coffee.”

cultureItems_html: Quality over quantity — Ship fewer games, but ship them right
Sustainable pace — No death marches. Go home at 5.
Weird is good — The best ideas sound dumb at first
Everyone codes — Even the Creative Director pushes commits
Bats are cool — The Congress Avenue Bridge colony is a tourist attraction for a reason
aboutTheStudio.yourRole_html: Congratulations — you’ve just been hired as a Junior Developer! You’ll be working alongside our senior team on a new client project. They’ll guide you through the development process, share their wisdom, and help you grow as a programmer. By the end of the semester, you’ll have shipped a real product.

meetTheTeam.label: Meet the Team
meetTheTeam.intro_html: These are the people who’ll guide you through your first game development project. Each brings unique expertise — and memorable advice.

members.name: Danny Cavanaugh
members.quote_html: “If it works but you don’t know why, it doesn’t work.”
members.bio_html: 10 years at EA, left after shipping Madden 2019 with an ulcer and a divorce. Moved to Austin, found peace, started Bat City. Calm, methodical, speaks in debugging metaphors.

members.name: Sasha Mireles
members.quote_html: “Every function tells a story. What’s yours saying?”
members.bio_html: Film school to indie games. Directed three award-winning mobile games before joining. Passionate, expressive, speaks with her hands. Gets genuinely excited about player experience.

members.name: Terrence “T-Bone” Booker
members.quote_html: “My job is to play your game wrong. On purpose. Aggressively.”
members.bio_html: Former touring musician (bass player, hence “T-Bone”), pivoted to QA testing at 35. Loves finding bugs more than playing bass. Gleefully destructive — takes genuine joy in breaking things.

members.name: Priya Venkatesh
members.quote_html: “The deadline is the deadline. Let’s figure out what’s achievable.”
members.bio_html: CS degree from UT Austin, worked at Dell before realizing she was better at organizing developers than being one. Unflappable. Quietly confident. Has never missed a deadline.

members.name: Tyler Nguyen
members.quote_html: “Dude, I Googled that same error like six times. Here’s what finally worked.”
members.bio_html: Just graduated from Texas State. This is his first job — he’s six months ahead of you. Earnest, helpful, still remembers what it’s like to not know things.
